title: musical-organogram-represents
prompt: Envision the Psychic Saxophone: keys that respond to brainwave frequencies for microtonal adjustments...
answer: This organogram represents a simple musical instrument with an acoustic component (circle) and an interface component (rectangle). The acoustic shape symbolizes the resonant space, while the interface indicates how performers interact with it.
summary: This organogram represents a simple musical instrument with an acoustic component (circle) and an interface component (rectangle). The acoustic shape symbolizes the resonant space, while the interface indicates how performers interact with it.
materials_text:
1. Set of 12 phosphor bronze strings
2. Carbon fiber body
3. Vulcanized rubber gaskets
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
plt.style.use('dark_background')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['left'].set_color('black')
ax.spines['bottom'].set_color('black')
ax.grid(True, color='grey', alpha=0.3)

# Acoustic shape (circle) - wood
acoustic_shape = plt.Circle((2, 1), radius=0.5, fill=True, color='orange', alpha=0.8)
ax.add_patch(acoustic_shape)

# Interface (rectangle) - plastic
interface = plt.Rectangle((4, 0.5), width=1, height=1, fill=True, color='grey', alpha=0.7)
ax.add_patch(interface)

plt.show()
plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
plt.style.use('dark_background')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['left'].set_color('black')
ax.spines['bottom'].set_color('black')
ax.grid(True, color='grey', alpha=0.3)

# Acoustic shape (circle) - wood
acoustic_shape = plt.Circle((2, 1), radius=0.5, fill=True, color='orange', alpha=0.8)
ax.add_patch(acoustic_shape)

# Interface (rectangle) - plastic
interface = plt.Rectangle((4, 0.5), width=1, height=1, fill=True, color='grey', alpha=0.7)
ax.add_patch(interface)

plt.show()
stl_code:
import numpy as np
import trimesh

# map: air-column -> cylinder, bell radiation -> cone, mouthpiece interface -> cylinder
body = trimesh.creation.cylinder(radius=17.29, height=171.3, sections=48)
bell = trimesh.creation.cone(radius=25.82, height=45.53, sections=48)
bell.apply_translation((0, 0, -108.42))
mouth = trimesh.creation.cylinder(radius=9.04, height=28.39, sections=48)
mouth.apply_translation((0, 0, 99.84))
spine = trimesh.creation.box(extents=(13.83, 19.02, 68.52))
mesh = trimesh.util.concatenate([body, bell, mouth, spine])
